reason: Immediate remediation requires reverting the edge rule 'geo-restrict-v42' from blocking to dry-run mode, but Meridian does not have direct access to production edge policy at StratosNetworks—the external provider. The change needed is urgent (sev-1, major outage), of minimal risk, and narrowly scoped: revert to previous config commit (d7f0a5c2) to restore legitimate customer traffic, as dry-run mode preserves observability and disables the false positive blocking. A broader rollback (full rule removal) would add compliance risk and requires executive approval.
scope_constraint: Provider (StratosNetworks) is authorized only to revert 'geo-restrict-v42' from blocking to dry-run mode across all production edge locations (restore exactly previous config, commit d7f0a5c2). No further edge policy changes, broader rule removals, or geo-classification model updates to be performed at this time. Action limited to targeted, low-risk rollback to restore customer traffic; further changes must be separately reviewed.
